title: Curd Rice (Thayir Sadam)
description: Curd Rice (Thayir Sadam) is a comforting South Indian rice dish made with cooked rice, fresh curd, and a flavorful tempering of spices. Creamy, refreshing, and easy to digest, it is often served as the final course of a traditional meal.

ingredients.title: For the Rice
items: 1 cup Basmati or Ponni rice (short-grain is preferable)

ingredients.title: For Tempering (Tadka)
items: 1–2 green chilies finely chopped (optional extra spice)
1–2 small fresh red chilies (optional, for color)

ingredients.title: For Garnish
items: 1–2 tbsp pomegranate seeds (optional, for color and freshness)
1 tbsp grated carrot (optional, lightly sautéed or raw)

instruction.title: Step 1: Cook the Rice
steps: Rinse rice thoroughly until water runs clear.
Cook rice in 2 cups water with ½ tsp salt until soft, slightly mushy but not sticky.
Tip: Rice should be slightly overcooked so it blends well with curd.
Spread rice on a plate or tray to cool slightly.

instruction.title: Step 2: Prepare the Curd Mixture
steps: Mix in milk for creaminess (optional) and adjust salt.
Fold the rice gently into the curd until evenly mixed.
Tip: Avoid over-mixing; a light, fluffy texture is ideal.

instruction.title: Step 3: Tempering (Tadka)
steps: Heat ghee in a small pan.
Add mustard seeds; let them splutter.
Add urad dal, chana dal, curry leaves, green chilies, and dried red chilies.
Add grated ginger and a pinch of hing; sauté for 20–30 seconds until aromatic.
Pour the hot tempering over the curd rice and mix gently.

instruction.title: Step 4: Garnish & Serve
steps: Garnish with chopped coriander, pomegranate seeds, and grated carrot.
Serve immediately at room temperature or chilled for a refreshing taste.

servingSuggestions.title: Serving Suggestions
items: Serve with pickle or papad for a complete meal.
Enjoy at room temperature or chilled.
Pairs well with mango pickle, fried chilies, or potato fry.

chefTips: Use freshly homemade curd for best flavor.
Slightly warm the rice before mixing with curd so it absorbs the yogurt evenly.
Tempering in ghee gives a richer aroma than oil.
Garnishes like pomegranate and lightly fried curry leaves add visual appeal.
Serve with pickle or papad for a complete meal.
